title: Add standalone CLI
description:
The portfolio philosophy is that tools should work both as CLI and as plugin — not just one or the other. Z Spec is currently plugin-only. A standalone CLI wrapping fuzz and probcli with the plugin's ProB compatibility rules would be useful independently of Claude Code.

Minimum CLI surface:
- z-spec check <file> — type-check with fuzz
- z-spec test <file> — animate/model-check with probcli
- z-spec doctor — verify dependencies (see related bead)

The CLI would apply the same ProB compatibility transforms (ZBOOL, bounded integers, flat schemas) that the plugin commands encode in their prompts, but as deterministic code.
close_reason: Not pursuing — plugin-only is the right model for Z Spec

title: Add doctor command
description:
Biff, Quarry, and LangLearn TTS all have doctor commands that verify installation health. Z Spec depends on fuzz (compiled from source) and probcli (platform-specific binary with Tcl/Tk dependency) — exactly the kind of dependencies that break.

Add a /z doctor command (or extend /z setup) that reports pass/fail for:
- fuzz binary found and executable
- fuzz.sty installed for LaTeX
- probcli binary found and executable
- Tcl/Tk libraries available for probcli
close_reason: Implemented /z doctor command with prod+dev variants, updated help files and README

title: Add graphviz and xdot to install.sh
description: probcli's -dot flag generates DOT files for state space visualization, but the viewer toolchain isn't installed by install.sh. Add graphviz (brew install graphviz) and xdot (pipx install xdot) to the setup process. Note: xdot must be installed via pipx, not uv tool — PyGObject needs Homebrew's native GLib/GTK dylibs at runtime. See docs/research/graphviz-install.md for the full install chain and gotchas.
close_reason: Superseded by claude-z-spec-plugin-7bf (Lux interactive state explorer). Graphviz/xdot are no longer needed — lux renders state graphs natively via draw canvas, eliminating the external DOT viewer toolchain.

title: Add version to plugin.json
description:
plugin.json is missing the version field. All other plugins declare a version:
- PR/FAQ: 1.0.0
- Biff: 0.3.0
- Dungeon: 0.1.0

Add version field to plugin.json for consistency.
close_reason: version field in marketplace.json, pinned to ref v0.1.1

title: Lux Phase 1b: counter-example trace visualizer
description:
When lux is enabled and probcli finds an invariant violation, render the counter-example trace as a lux diagram.

## Deliverable
Skill prompt enhancement to /z-spec:test. When a counter-example is found AND displayMode=dmOn, compose a show_diagram() scene showing the trace.

## Diagram Layout
Linear chain, top to bottom:
- Layers: one per step in the trace
- Nodes: state ID + key variable values (2-3 most relevant vars)
- Edges: operation name + parameters
- Final node: red background, broken invariant text below

## Data Source
Parse probcli -model_check stdout when COUNTER EXAMPLE FOUND:
- Step lines: 'N: OperationName(params)'
- State dump: 'STATE = (var1=val1 & var2=val2 & ...)'

## Implementation
Only renders when violation exists. Otherwise dashboard (Phase 1a) shows 'No counter-example found.' The LLM parses the trace, maps steps to layers/nodes/edges, calls show_diagram().

## Prerequisite
Phase 1a (dashboard provides the container; trace is an additional tab or scene)

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 1b
close_reason: Closed

title: Z spec Layer 2: Biff workflow state model
description:
Extend the Claude Code base Z spec (examples/claude-code.tex) with biff workflow state.

## State Extension
- planStatus : ZBOOL (manual plan set via /plan)
- planSource : PlanSource (manual | auto)
- mesgMode : ZBOOL (accepting messages)
- unreadCount : nat (unread inbox messages)
- talkActive : ZBOOL (in /talk session)
- wallActive : ZBOOL (wall post visible)

## Key Invariants to Verify
1. Plan before work: toolPhase \!= tpNone AND currentTool in {Edit,Write,Bash} => planStatus = ztrue
2. Read before respond: unreadCount > 0 => Stop hook injects 'you have unread messages'
3. Wall awareness: wallActive = ztrue at SessionStart => additionalContext includes wall

## Operations to Add (~6)
- SetPlan (PostToolUse on Bash auto from git branch, or manual /plan)
- SendMessage (PostToolUse on mcp__biff__write)
- ReadMessages (PostToolUse on mcp__biff__read_messages)
- ReceiveMessage (external NATS notification)
- StartTalk / EndTalk (PostToolUse on mcp__biff__talk / talk_end)
- PostWall / ClearWall (PostToolUse on mcp__biff__wall)

## Approach
- Compose with base State schema via schema inclusion
- Keep relay/inbox internals opaque — model only boolean flags visible to hooks
- Target: fuzz type-check + probcli model-check
close_reason: Model-checked: 161K states, 789K transitions, ALL visited, no counter-example. Key property proven: plan+bead required before Edit/Write/Bash.

title: Font coverage: add SMP glyph support for mathematical double-struck characters
description: Characters outside the Basic Multilingual Plane (U+0000–FFFF) render as replacement glyphs (◆) in lux. Specifically, 𝔽 (U+1D53D, Mathematical Double-Struck Capital F) used for Z notation \\finset does not render. The current font stack (Arial Unicode + Apple Symbols on macOS) lacks SMP coverage. Needs a merge font with Mathematical Alphanumeric Symbols block (U+1D400–1D7FF) — e.g. STIX Two Math or Noto Sans Math.
close_reason: Wrong repo — moved to lux

title: Lux integration: interactive state explorer
description:
Lux integration: interactive state explorer for ProB animation.

Architecture: Option D hybrid — z-spec-specific skill prompt + Lux data-explorer template.

The z-spec plugin creates a skill (e.g. /z:explore or auto-activated during /z test) that composes Lux elements into an interactive state explorer:

1. State graph — draw canvas rendering ProB's DOT output as an interactive graph. Nodes are circles/rects (state IDs), edges are labeled arrows (operations). Clickable nodes via recv() to inspect state variables. Highlighted path for counter-example traces. Adapts the architecture diagram Lux skill.

2. Operation panel — button elements for each available operation, grayed out (disabled) when preconditions fail in the current state. Clicking an operation steps the animation forward. recv() captures clicks.

3. Invariant dashboard — table element showing each invariant with green/red status per state. Filterable to show only failing invariants. Adapts the data-explorer Lux skill.

4. Counter-example visualizer — when ProB finds an invariant violation, a step-by-step walkthrough: sequence of state boxes connected by operation arrows, with the violating state highlighted in red and the broken invariant shown.

5. Partition test matrix — TTF test derivation table as an interactive sortable/filterable table (data-explorer skill). Columns: operation, partition class, test predicate, coverage status.

Delivery mechanism:
- Skill prompt in z-spec plugin encodes the explorer layout as JSON templates
- Reuses Lux data-explorer and architecture-diagram patterns
- ProB already outputs DOT format — the skill translates DOT nodes/edges to draw canvas commands
- MCP instructions provide formatting rules via Prior-Context Priming
close_reason: Decomposed into 7 phased beads: Phase 1 (dm1, 49o, k0o) — static dashboard, counter-example visualizer, partition matrix. Phase 2 (pl3, 7ie, 8mj) — state graph, operation panel, invariant list. Phase 3 (vpr) — interactive stepping. Research at docs/research-lux-state-explorer.md, roadmap at docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md.

title: Lux Phase 2b: operation panel with enabled/disabled state
description:
Button panel showing available Z operations, with disabled state derived from the DOT edge list.

## Deliverable
Part of the state explorer scene. One button per Z operation. For the selected state node, buttons are enabled/disabled based on whether the DOT graph has an outgoing edge for that operation.

## Data Source
DOT edge list: if state N has edge 'N -> M [label=OpName]', then OpName is enabled in state N. No probcli eval needed — the DOT graph is precomputed ground truth.

## Implementation
Parse DOT once, build adjacency map: state_id → set of operation names. On state selection (recv()), update button disabled flags.

## Prerequisite
Phase 2a (state graph provides the state selection context)

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 2b

title: Lux Phase 2c: invariant list from LaTeX parsing
description:
Static table of invariants extracted from the Z spec State schema.

## Deliverable
Table element with columns: #, Invariant (from comment), Predicate (LaTeX text), Status (PASS for model-checked specs).

## Data Source
Parse the \begin{schema}{State} ... \where ... \end{schema} block. Each line after \where that contains a predicate becomes a row. Comments above predicates become invariant names.

## Implementation
Regex parsing of LaTeX. Static table — per-state evaluation deferred to Phase 3.

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 2c

title: Z spec Layer 2: Vox notification state model
description:
Extend the Claude Code base Z spec with vox notification/speech state.

## State Extension
- notifyMode : NotifyMode (off | onDemand | continuous)
- speakMode : ZBOOL (y=TTS / n=chimes only)
- vibeMode : VibeMode (auto | manual | off)
- stopHookActive : ZBOOL (prevents re-entry in Stop decision-block)

## Key Invariants to Verify
1. Stop hook decision-block: no infinite loop — stopHookActive = ztrue => FinishResponse proceeds
2. Notification requires enabled: notifyMode = off => notification hooks are no-ops
3. Speak requires notify: speakMode = ztrue => notifyMode != off

## Operations to Add (~4)
- EnableNotify / DisableNotify (PostToolUse on mcp__vox__notify)
- Mute / Unmute (PostToolUse on mcp__vox__speak)
- StopHookSpeak (models vox decision-block pattern on Stop)
- AccumulateSignal (PostToolUse on Bash, appends to vibeSignals)

## Approach
- Low complexity: 4 operations, 4 state variables
- Key value: formally verify the Stop hook re-entry pattern (block once, allow on second fire)
- Target: fuzz type-check + probcli model-check
close_reason: Model-checked: 11K states, 48K transitions, ALL visited. Key property: no infinite Stop loop. Found and fixed invariant bug during verification.

title: Add curl | bash installer
description:
Z Spec is the only plugin with no install path. Every other plugin (PR/FAQ, Biff, Dungeon) has a curl | bash installer that clones to ~/.claude/plugins/local-plugins/plugins/, registers in marketplace.json, and verifies dependencies.

Pattern to follow: prfaq/install.sh or claude-dungeon/install.sh.

The installer should:
1. Clone the repo to ~/.claude/plugins/local-plugins/plugins/z-spec
2. Register in marketplace.json
3. Check for fuzz and probcli (or prompt to run /z setup)
4. Clear plugin cache
close_reason: install.sh added in v0.1.1 — POSIX sh, SSH fallback, three-tier install in README

title: Z spec Layer 2: Lux visual surface state model
description:
Extend the Claude Code base Z spec with lux visual output surface state. This model needs further design — lux has more coupling potential than initially apparent.

## Known State Extension (minimal)
- sceneActive : ZBOOL (a scene is displayed)
- luxEnabled : ZBOOL (lux window is spawned)
- pendingInteraction : ZBOOL (recv() blocking for user input)

## Integration Points to Brainstorm
Before building, enumerate all the ways lux should integrate with the workflow:

### Beads Integration
- Beads board displayed when user works with beads (bd ready, bd list)
- Board auto-updates as issues transition (in_progress, closed)
- Detail panel for selected bead

### PR Workflow
- PR widget showing PR status, checks, review comments
- Auto-display on mcp__github__create_pull_request
- Update on check completion, review submission

### Vox Complement
- Visual summaries complement voice summaries at Stop
- Dashboard displayed alongside /recap narration

### Quarry Integration
- Search results displayed as interactive table
- Document detail panel on selection

### Session Dashboard
- Git status, active beads, unread messages, session timeline
- Auto-update on state changes

### Test/Build Status
- Test results table after test runs
- Build status with pass/fail indicators

## Approach
- Build last — depends on understanding all other Layer 2 models first
- Brainstorm full integration list before modeling
- Likely higher complexity than other models due to scene graph state
- Target: fuzz type-check + probcli model-check
close_reason: Model-checked: 10K states, 56K transitions, ALL visited. 6 invariants proven. Consumer integration patterns documented for beads, quarry, PR, z-spec, session dashboard. 4 beads created in lux.

title: Cherry-pick /z contracts, /z oracle, /z refine from ebowman/z-spec
description: Cherry-pick commit d657ac8 from ebowman/z-spec. Adds three refinement gap commands: /z contracts (generate design-by-contract annotations), /z oracle (generate test oracles from Z predicates), /z refine (verify refinement between abstract and concrete specifications). No new external dependencies. Adds commands/contracts.md, commands/oracle.md, commands/refine.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: Lux Phase 1a: model check results dashboard in /z-spec:test
description:
When lux is enabled, /z-spec:test renders model check results as a lux dashboard instead of text.

## Deliverable
Skill prompt enhancement to /z-spec:test. When displayMode=dmOn, compose a show_dashboard() scene after probcli runs.

## Dashboard Layout
- Metric cards: States Analysed, Transitions Fired, Operations Covered (N/M), Result (PASS/FAIL)
- Operation coverage table: columns [Operation, Times Fired, Status]. Filters: search by name, combo for covered/uncovered.
- Title: '<spec-name> — Model Check Results'

## Data Source
Parse probcli -model_check stdout:
- 'States analysed: N' → metric card
- 'Transitions fired: N' → metric card  
- Coverage from -coverage flag or animation output
- 'No counter example found' / 'COUNTER EXAMPLE FOUND' → result card (green/red)

## Implementation
~40 lines of data mapping in the skill prompt. The LLM runs probcli, parses output, composes JSON, calls show_dashboard(). Zero lux code changes.

## Prerequisite
lux-t1p (display mode toggle) — skill checks lux availability before composing.

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 1a
docs/research-lux-state-explorer.md
close_reason: Closed

title: B-Method support (AMN, refinement, code generation)
description:
Add B-Method (Abstract Machine Notation) support alongside Z. B shares the same mathematical foundations but adds a first-class refinement chain (Abstract Machine → Refinement → Implementation) with structured proof obligations and code generation.

Key capabilities:
- B abstract machine authoring or translation from Z specs
- Refinement chain with REFINES clauses and obligation generation
- Code generation from Implementation machines (C, Ada, Java)
- ProB is already in the toolchain (probcli backend for /z test) and supports both Z and B natively

This is the spec-to-verified-code pipeline that Z alone cannot provide. The Paris Metro Line 14 safety-critical software was built this way — B specs refined to implementation with machine-checked proofs at every step.
close_reason: Shipped: 4 B commands, 2 reference files, 4 PRDs, 8 modified files. All markdown passes lint.

title: Cherry-pick /z prove from ebowman/z-spec
description: Cherry-pick commit b93e8a9 from ebowman/z-spec. Adds /z prove command for Lean 4 proof obligations — generates Lean 4 files from Z specifications and uses lake build to machine-check them. Introduces new dependency: elan (Lean version manager), lean (theorem prover), lake (build system), Mathlib (~2GB). Adds commands/prove.md, commands/prove-dev.md, reference/lean4-patterns.md, and updates commands/setup.md and commands/doctor.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: Z spec Layer 2: Quarry knowledge capture state model
description:
Extend the Claude Code base Z spec with quarry automatic knowledge capture state.

## State Extension
- codebaseIndexed : ZBOOL (SessionStart auto-registered and synced)
- activeDatabase : ZBOOL (non-default DB selected via /use)
- captureEnabled (3 flags):
  - sessionSync : ZBOOL
  - webFetch : ZBOOL
  - compaction : ZBOOL
- lastCompactionSaved : ZBOOL (PreCompact captured session notes)

## Key Invariants to Verify
1. Knowledge available before work: spProcessing => codebaseIndexed = ztrue
2. No knowledge loss on compaction: PreCompact fires AND compaction=ztrue => lastCompactionSaved'=ztrue
3. WebFetch auto-capture: PostToolUse on WebFetch when webFetch=ztrue => URL captured

## Operations to Add (~5)
- IndexCodebase (SessionStart hook auto-sync)
- CaptureWebFetch (PostToolUse on WebFetch)
- CapturePreCompact (PreCompact hook saves session notes)
- SwitchDatabase (PostToolUse on mcp__quarry__use)
- SearchKnowledge (PostToolUse on mcp__quarry__find, injects context)

## Approach
- Moderate complexity: 5 operations, ~6 state variables
- Most interesting invariant: 'no knowledge loss on compaction'
- Target: fuzz type-check + probcli model-check
close_reason: Model-checked: 5K states, 23K transitions, ALL visited. Found critical wiring gap: 3 Python handlers never invoked. 4 beads created in quarry.

title: Add plugin dev/prod namespace isolation
description:
Adopt the --plugin-dir dev/prod isolation pattern from punt-kit (PR #15).

## What

- Change plugin.json name from z-spec to z-spec-dev
- Add -dev command variants for any existing commands
- Add release/restore scripts (scripts/release-plugin.sh, scripts/restore-dev-plugin.sh)

## Reference

- punt-kit PR #15: https://github.com/punt-labs/punt-kit/pull/15
- Standard: punt-kit/standards/plugins.md Dev/Prod Namespace Isolation

No MCP server — commands only.
close_reason: Dev/prod namespace isolation shipped in v0.1.0 — release-plugin.sh and restore-dev-plugin.sh scripts

title: Lux Phase 1c: TTF partition test matrix display
description:
When lux is enabled, /z-spec:partition renders the test derivation table as a lux data explorer.

## Deliverable
Skill prompt enhancement to /z-spec:partition. When displayMode=dmOn, compose a show_table() scene with the partition matrix.

## Table Layout
Columns: Operation, Partition Class, Test Predicate, Coverage Status
Filters: search by operation name, combo by coverage (covered/uncovered/all)
Detail panel: full predicate text + partition derivation explanation

## Data Source
/z-spec:partition already produces this data as text. The skill wraps it in show_table() JSON.

## Prerequisite
lux-t1p (display mode toggle)

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 1c
close_reason: Closed

title: Add /z-spec:explain and /z-spec:tutor commands
description:
## Summary

Implement the explain/tutor command pair for z-spec, per the org standard (punt-kit-scd).

## /z-spec:explain <topic>

Apply formal methods expertise to explain any topic:
- Identify state, invariants, operations in the topic
- Show how Z notation would model it
- Contrast informal vs formal reasoning about the topic
- Example: `/z-spec:explain microservices` → state partitioning, interface contracts, refinement

## /z-spec:tutor

Teach formal methods from novice to fluency:
- Z notation fundamentals (sets, relations, schemas)
- Schema calculus and composition
- Refinement and proof obligations
- State machines and temporal properties
- Track mastery in `.claude/z-spec.local.md` (ALEKS-style)

## Integration

- **Vox**: check `.vox/config.md` speak state; explain/tutor aloud when enabled
- **Persona**: auto-set "formal methods mentor" on tutor entry; read persona at L3 for explain
- Graceful degradation when vox/persona absent

## Depends on

punt-kit-scd (org standard for explain/tutor commands)

title: Integrate Quarry for domain knowledge during spec generation
description:
PR/FAQ already uses Quarry as an optional dependency to enhance its research phase. Z Spec should do the same — when Quarry is installed, commands like /z create and /z code2model could search the user's indexed documents for domain knowledge (requirements docs, design docs, API specs, prior specifications) to produce more accurate Z specifications.

Use case: a user has indexed their project's design documentation, API contracts, and requirements in Quarry. When they run /z create, the plugin searches Quarry for relevant domain context before generating the spec. This helps produce specifications that use correct domain terminology, capture real invariants, and reflect actual business rules rather than guessing from code alone.

Similarly, /z elaborate could pull narrative from indexed design docs rather than requiring the user to point at specific files.

Pattern to follow: PR/FAQ's researcher agent checks for Quarry MCP tools and uses search_documents when available, falling back gracefully when Quarry is not installed.
close_reason: z-specification Quarry collection registered with 106 docs, CLAUDE.md mandates Quarry use

title: Lux Phase 2a: state graph visualization from ProB DOT output
description:
Render ProB's DOT state space as an interactive lux diagram.

## Deliverable
Skill prompt (or sub-skill) that parses probcli -dot output and renders via show_diagram().

## For small specs (≤100 states)
- Parse DOT nodes: extract state ID, variable values from label
- Parse DOT edges: extract source, target, operation name
- Map to show_diagram(): layers grouped by session phase, nodes with state IDs, edges with operation labels

## For large specs (>100 states)
- Cluster by session phase (one node per cluster)
- Show transition counts between clusters
- Defer expand-on-click to Phase 3

## Scale Data
Vox spec: 11,452 nodes, 52,842 edges, 87K-line DOT file. Needs clustering.
Search-panel spec: ~50 states. Works directly with show_diagram().

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 2a
docs/research-lux-state-explorer.md

title: SVG export pipeline for Z schemas (LaTeX → PDF → SVG)
description:
Add a command (e.g. /z-spec:export-svg) that renders individual Z schemas as SVG files suitable for embedding in web pages. Pipeline: spec.tex → pdflatex + fuzz.sty → PDF → pdf2svg → per-schema SVGs. Use case: displaying Z specifications as native content on punt-labs.com rather than linking to PDFs. Each schema is a self-contained visual unit — SVGs are resolution-independent and can carry alt text for accessibility.

Implementation note: \\pagebreak between schemas in the LaTeX source gives each schema its own PDF page, making per-schema extraction via pdf2svg trivial (one page = one SVG).
close_reason: No longer needed: lux now has sufficient font support to render Z schemas and mathematical notation natively. The SVG export pipeline was designed for web embedding, but lux's display server renders schemas directly at 60fps with proper typesetting.

title: Explore: push z-spec functionality into deterministic code paths (L1 tools for L4 agents)
description:
Inspired by https://www.punt-labs.com/blog/building-l1-tools-for-l4-agents

## Current State
z-spec is a pure plugin — all prompt-driven skills, no runtime code. The LLM generates LaTeX Z specs from scratch every time (L4). The only L1 tools are external binaries (fuzz, probcli). The verification gap exists in every line of LaTeX the LLM writes.

## The Opportunity
Apply the Lux boundary pattern: the LLM describes WHAT (state variables, invariants, operations as structured data), deterministic code produces HOW (syntactically correct LaTeX, valid hook wiring, passing type-checks).

## Candidate L1 Tools

### Spec Generation (reduce LLM to data mapping)
- **Template engine**: Takes structured input (state vars, free types, operations, invariants) as JSON/YAML and produces syntactically correct fuzz-compatible LaTeX. The LLM fills in a data structure; the tool writes correct syntax. Eliminates classes of bugs: \\t1 vs \\quad~, missing frame conditions, inconsistent primed variables.
- **Hook-to-Z translator**: Reads hooks.json + hooks.py and auto-generates the Hook Event Map section and skeleton operation schemas. The LLM refines invariants and preconditions; the tool handles boilerplate.

### Validation (catch errors before model-checking)
- **Convention checker**: Lint Z specs for project conventions — bounded sets, ZBOOL usage, no \\t1, frame conditions present, all primed variables assigned. Faster than probcli, catches common mistakes immediately.
- **Coverage auditor**: Compare hooks.json registrations against Z spec operations. Flag dead handlers (handler exists, no registration) and orphan operations (operation exists, no handler). This is the audit that found quarry's wiring gap — automate it.

### Code Generation (from spec to implementation)
- **Hook scaffold generator**: Given a Z spec with PreToolUse/Stop/PostToolUse operations, generate the hooks.json entries, shell dispatcher scripts, and Python handler skeletons. The LLM fills in business logic; the tool handles wiring.
- **Test generator**: From Z spec operation preconditions, generate pytest test cases that verify the handler respects the preconditions. Each \\where clause becomes a test assertion.

## The Ratio Target
Like Lux (40 lines agentic / 2000 lines deterministic), aim for:
- LLM writes: state variable names, invariant predicates, operation semantics (the WHAT)
- Tool writes: LaTeX syntax, frame conditions, hooks.json, shell scripts, Python boilerplate (the HOW)

## Design Questions
1. Should the structured input be JSON, YAML, or a DSL?
2. Should the template engine be a Python CLI (following cli.md) or a prompt-driven skill that calls MCP tools?
3. Which candidate tool has the highest ROI? (Convention checker is probably lowest effort, highest immediate value)
4. Does this change z-spec from a pure plugin to a CLI+plugin hybrid?

## References
- Blog post: building-l1-tools-for-l4-agents.md
- Lux architecture: 22 built-in elements, LLM writes data mapping only
- Current z-spec audit findings: \\t1 bug, ghost states, context overflow — all preventable by L1 tooling
- cli.md projection strategy: CLI is source of truth, MCP/plugin projects it

title: Lux Phase 2d: render Z spec schemas in a Spec tab
description:
Add a Spec tab to lux displays that renders the .tex zed blocks as formatted Z notation using Unicode math symbols. Parse \begin{schema}/\begin{zed} blocks from the .tex source and render declarations, predicates, and free types with proper Z characters. This gives a readable view of the spec alongside test/partition results without requiring a PDF viewer.

## Implementation Prompt

You are implementing bead claude-z-spec-plugin-vbz: render Z spec schemas as Unicode text in a lux Spec tab.

### Context

The z-spec plugin has two lux-enabled skills: /z-spec:test (commands/test.md, test-dev.md) and /z-spec:partition (commands/partition.md, partition-dev.md). Both render results dashboards. This bead adds a "Spec" tab that shows the Z specification itself, rendered as readable Unicode math — no PDF viewer needed.

### Rendering constraints

**BMP only.** Lux uses Arial Unicode + Apple Symbols on macOS. Characters outside the Basic Multilingual Plane (U+0000–FFFF) render as replacement glyphs. All Unicode symbols in the translation table below are BMP-safe. Specifically: use `F` for `\finset` (not `𝔽` U+1D53D which is SMP). See lux bead `lux-ci8` for the underlying font coverage issue.

**Open-right schema boxes.** Lux text elements use proportional font, so right-side box-drawing characters will not align. Use left-open boxes only — no right border (`┐`, `┤`, `┘`, or trailing `│`).

**Long top lines.** The top rule (`┌─ Name ─...`) must be ~5 `─` characters LONGER than the `├` and `└` rules. The schema name is rendered in proportional font, which is wider than the equivalent count of `─` characters. The `├`/`└` rules (all `─`) are the true content width baseline. The top line must compensate for the proportional name text by adding extra dashes:

```
┌─ SchemaName ────────────────────────────────────────────────
│ var1 : Type1
│ var2 : Type2
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
│ predicate1
│ predicate2
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Note: the `├`/`└` lines are the same length. The `┌` line is visually longer to compensate for the name. All rules should overshoot the content — better long than short.

**Collapsing headers.** Group schemas under `collapsing_header` elements by `\section{}` from the .tex source. State and types sections should be `default_open: true`. Operations section should be `default_open: false` (can be many schemas).

### What to build

Add a new step to test.md, test-dev.md, partition.md, and partition-dev.md that:

1. **Parses the .tex source** to extract Z blocks:
   - `\begin{schema}{Name}` ... `\end{schema}` — named schemas with declaration/predicate parts separated by `\where`
   - `\begin{zed}` ... `\end{zed}` — standalone definitions, free types, axiomatic boxes
   - `\begin{gendef}` ... `\end{gendef}` — generic definitions

2. **Converts LaTeX Z commands to Unicode**. Build a translation table in the prompt (not code — this is a skill prompt, not application code). Key mappings:

   | LaTeX | Unicode | Meaning |
   |-------|---------|---------|
   | `\nat` | ℕ | natural numbers |
   | `\num` | ℤ | integers |
   | `\real` | ℝ | reals |
   | `\power` | ℙ | power set |
   | `\finset` | F | finite power set (plain F — 𝔽 U+1D53D is SMP, see lux-ci8) |
   | `\seq` | seq | sequence |
   | `\cross` | × | Cartesian product |
   | `\fun` / `\pfun` | → / ⇸ | total/partial function |
   | `\bij` / `\pinj` / `\surj` | ⤖ / ⤔ / ↠ | bijection/partial injection/surjection |
   | `\rel` | ↔ | relation |
   | `\in` | ∈ | membership |
   | `\notin` | ∉ | non-membership |
   | `\subseteq` | ⊆ | subset |
   | `\subset` | ⊂ | proper subset |
   | `\cup` / `\cap` | ∪ / ∩ | union/intersection |
   | `\setminus` | ∖ | set difference |
   | `\emptyset` | ∅ | empty set |
   | `\langle` / `\rangle` | ⟨ / ⟩ | angle brackets (sequences) |
   | `\forall` / `\exists` | ∀ / ∃ | quantifiers |
   | `\land` / `\lor` | ∧ / ∨ | logical and/or |
   | `\lnot` / `\neg` | ¬ | negation |
   | `\implies` / `\iff` | ⇒ / ⇔ | implication/biconditional |
   | `\Delta` / `\Xi` | Δ / Ξ | delta/xi (state change/no change) |
   | `\dom` / `\ran` | dom / ran | domain/range |
   | `\dres` / `\rres` | ◁ / ▷ | domain/range restriction |
   | `\ndres` / `\nrres` | ⩤ / ⩥ | domain/range anti-restriction |
   | `\oplus` | ⊕ | function override |
   | `\mapsto` | ↦ | maplet |
   | `\neq` | ≠ | not equal |
   | `\leq` / `\geq` | ≤ / ≥ | less/greater or equal |
   | `\#` | # | cardinality |
   | `\\` | (newline) | line break in predicates |
   | `\also` | (blank line) | separator between schema parts |
   | `\where` | ├──── | horizontal rule (open-right, left-connected) |
   | `\hide` | \ | schema hiding |
   | `\project` | ↾ | schema projection |
   | `\semi` | ⨟ | schema composition |
   | `\pipe` | ≫ | schema piping |
   | `\theta` | θ | theta (binding) |
   | `\_1` | ₁ | subscript digits |
   | `?` suffix | ? | input decoration (keep as-is) |
   | `!` suffix | ! | output decoration (keep as-is) |
   | `'` suffix | ′ | prime (after-state) |

3. **Renders each schema as a lux text element** using the open-right box style described above. Use `kind: "text"` elements. Group under `collapsing_header` by section. Ensure top/middle/bottom rules are generously long (60+ `─` characters).

4. **Adds the Spec tab** to the existing `tab_bar` structure. In test.md/test-dev.md, the tab_bar already exists for counter-example traces — add Spec as the last tab. In partition.md/partition-dev.md, wrap the existing display in a tab_bar with "Partitions" and "Spec" tabs.

### Implementation approach

This is ALL skill prompt changes — no application code. The skill prompt instructs the LLM to:
- Read the .tex file (it already does this)
- Extract and convert Z blocks to Unicode using the translation table
- Build lux text elements with open-right box-drawing schema rendering
- Add them as a "Spec" tab in the dashboard

### Testing

After implementing, test with:
1. `/z-spec:test examples/claude-code.tex` — should show Spec tab with all 18 operation schemas
2. `/z-spec:partition examples/search-panel.tex` — should show Spec tab alongside partition matrix
3. Verify Unicode symbols render correctly in lux — all must be BMP (no replacement glyphs)
4. Verify open-right boxes render cleanly — top lines must extend past all content lines

### Files to modify

- `commands/test.md` — add Spec tab rendering step
- `commands/test-dev.md` — same changes (dev variant)
- `commands/partition.md` — add Spec tab, wrap in tab_bar
- `commands/partition-dev.md` — same changes (dev variant)
- `CHANGELOG.md` — add entry under Unreleased
close_reason: Merged PR #17

title: Lux Phase 3: interactive stepping and live invariant evaluation
description:
Full interactive Z specification debugger: click-to-step animation, per-state invariant evaluation, large graph navigation.

## Components
3a. Click-to-step: recv() captures operation button click → probcli eval → re-render
3b. Per-state invariant eval: evaluate Z predicates against selected state variables
3c. Large graph navigation: neighborhood view with expand-on-click, breadcrumbs

## Prerequisite
Phases 1-2 proven valuable. probcli REPL integration feasibility confirmed.

## Deferred deliberately
This is significant new work. The value of Phases 1-2 should be proven before investing in probcli as a live backend.

## Reference
docs/z-spec-lux-roadmap.md Phase 3

title: /z-spec:tutor — adaptive tutor for formal logic and Z notation
description: Add a /z-spec:tutor command that teaches formal logic and Z notation (set theory, predicate calculus, schema notation, refinement) through adaptive tutoring. Follow the AI-as-Tutor approach from Mollick & Mollick (2023) 'Assigning AI: Seven Approaches for Students, with Prompts' (Wharton/SSRN-4475995): guide through questions and explanations adapted to the learner's level, have learners explain in their own words, provide immediate feedback. Track mastery state across sessions (ALEKS-style Knowledge Space Theory). langlearn-tts already implements this pattern with language instructors. Part of the cross-cutting /tool:tutor philosophy — tools that teach, not just tools that do.
